citation: Bowhead call-type descriptors after Blackwell, Thode et al. 2015 (JASA 137:2398) and Clark & Johnson 1984.

0.name: Simple FM upsweep
0.desc: Short frequency-modulated call sweeping upward in frequency — the most common bowhead simple-call form.

1.name: Simple FM downsweep
1.desc: Frequency-modulated call sweeping downward; paired with upsweeps among the dominant simple calls.

2.name: Constant-frequency tonal
2.desc: Near-flat tonal call with little frequency modulation.

3.name: U-shaped (inflected)
3.desc: Down-then-up inflected contour — an undulated simple call.

5.name: Undulated / multi-inflected
5.desc: Several inflection points; a more modulated call contour.

6.name: Complex / pulsive
6.desc: Broadband, pulsive or harmonically rich complex call.

7.name: Song element
7.desc: Repeated, structured unit characteristic of bowhead song.
